title: Taking Charge of Your Fertility
author: Toni Weschler
subline: The definitive guide to natural birth control and pregnancy achievement.
customHTML:
<svg viewBox="0 0 1600 2400" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid slice" style="position:absolute;inset:0;width:100%;height:100%">
  <!-- A basal body temperature chart: 28 days of temperature readings. A clear red line shows the biphasic pattern (low, then spike at ovulation, then sustained high). The spike day is the only red. -->
  <g transform="translate(800 1100)" fill="none">
    <!-- Frame -->
    <line x1="-560" y1="-380" x2="-560" y2="220" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1"/>
    <line x1="-560" y1="220" x2="560" y2="220" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1"/>
    <!-- Day labels -->
    <g fill="#4c4c4c" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="11" font-weight="500" letter-spacing="0.18em" text-anchor="middle">
      <text x="-540" y="250">1</text>
      <text x="-340" y="250">7</text>
      <text x="-140" y="250">14</text>
      <text x="60" y="250">21</text>
      <text x="260" y="250">28</text>
    </g>
    <!-- Y axis -->
    <text x="-580" y="-380" text-anchor="end" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="11" font-weight="500" letter-spacing="0.18em" fill="#4c4c4c">99.0°</text>
    <text x="-580" y="-180" text-anchor="end" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="11" font-weight="500" letter-spacing="0.18em" fill="#4c4c4c">98.0°</text>
    <text x="-580" y="220" text-anchor="end" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="11" font-weight="500" letter-spacing="0.18em" fill="#4c4c4c">97.0°</text>
    <text x="-580" y="60" text-anchor="end" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="11" font-weight="500" letter-spacing="0.18em" fill="#4c4c4c">97.6°</text>
    <!-- Reference lines -->
    <line x1="-560" y1="60" x2="560" y2="60" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 4" opacity="0.6"/>
    <text x="540" y="50" text-anchor="end" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="11" font-weight="500" letter-spacing="0.18em" fill="#4c4c4c">COVER LINE</text>
    <!-- Pre-ovulation temperature — flat low, ash -->
    <path d="M-540 120 L -460 130 L -380 110 L -300 120 L -220 130 L -140 100" stroke="#cccccc" stroke-width="2"/>
    <!-- The OVULATION SPIKE — RED, sharp rise at day 14 -->
    <path d="M-140 100 L -100 -20 L -60 0 L -20 -10 L 20 -20 L 60 -40 L 100 -50 L 140 -40 L 180 -50 L 220 -40 L 260 -30 L 300 -40 L 340 -30 L 380 -20 L 420 -10 L 460 -20 L 500 -10 L 540 0" stroke="#fc1c46" stroke-width="3"/>
    <!-- Markers -->
    <g fill="#cccccc">
      <circle cx="-540" cy="120" r="3"/><circle cx="-460" cy="130" r="3"/><circle cx="-380" cy="110" r="3"/>
      <circle cx="-300" cy="120" r="3"/><circle cx="-220" cy="130" r="3"/><circle cx="-140" cy="100" r="3"/>
    </g>
    <!-- The ovulation marker — red dot at the spike -->
    <circle cx="-100" cy="-20" r="7" fill="#fc1c46"/>
    <text x="-100" y="-50" text-anchor="middle" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="13" font-weight="500" letter-spacing="0.18em" fill="#fc1c46">OVULATION</text>
  </g>
</svg>
rationale: A 28-day BBT (basal body temperature) chart: ash curve in the follicular phase, then a sharp red spike at day 14 (ovulation) and a sustained red plateau. The book is THE chart that taught millions of women to read their cycle. 320px title sits at the bottom. The single red = the spike and the post-ovulation high.
